ops.title: Statusline jj widgets were swapped to --ignore-working-copy custom-commands; their output lags unsnapshotted edits by design
ops.body:
On 2026-07-14 the two jj widgets in `~/.config/ccstatusline/settings.json` (jj-bookmarks, jj-changes) were replaced with `custom-command` widgets that cd to the workspace dir from the context JSON and run only `--ignore-working-copy` jj reads (`jj root`, `jj log …bookmarks`, `jj diff --stat`). Before that, every ~10s statusline refresh snapshotted the working copy of whatever repo the session sat in — a racer against interactive jj and a suspected contributor to the [[reposync-deployment]]-adjacent working-copy sweep incident.

**Why:** the built-in widgets hardcode non-IWC jj calls (ccstatusline 2.2.19 has no config knob); snapshots from background tools create concurrent-op races.

**How to apply:** if the statusline's `(+N,-M)` or 🔖 segment looks stale, that's the read-only lag — it updates only after something else snapshots (any user jj command). Don't "fix" it by removing `--ignore-working-copy`. Backup of the old config: `~/.config/ccstatusline/settings.json.bak-20260714`. On ccstatusline upgrades the settings survive (user config, not package data).
